description_long: This object is a traditional stone water well with a circular stone base and a cylindrical stone shaft. The well is topped with a pitched wooden roof made of reddish-brown wooden shingles, supported by two sturdy wooden posts. A horizontal wooden beam spans the posts, featuring a simple crank mechanism with a handle, likely for raising and lowering a bucket. The stonework is rugged and uneven, giving the well a rustic, hand-built appearance. The roof provides shelter for the well opening, and the stone base extends outward, forming a protective ring around the shaft. No decorative elements are visible, and the structure appears robust and utilitarian.
description: A rustic stone water well with a pitched wooden roof and a crank handle, featuring rugged stonework and a protective stone base.
description_view_0: Front view showing the well's circular stone base, wooden posts, pitched roof, and the crank handle clearly visible on the left.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the triangular profile of the roof and the depth of the stone shaft, with the crank handle partially visible.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the back of the pitched roof, the stone base, and the horizontal beam without the crank handle in view.
description_view_3: Opposite side view showing the roof's overhang and the stone base, with the crank handle on the far side and less visible.
attribute_presence_of_pulley_or_bucket_m: A simple crank handle mechanism is present, made of wood and metal, attached to the horizontal beam.
attribute_surrounding_protective_structu: The well is surrounded by a circular stone wall and topped with a pitched wooden roof supported by wooden posts.
